title: AI and Teaching – Duke Kunshan Center for Teaching and Learning
text:
AI and Teaching
The DKU Dual-Mode AI Fluency Framework
Beyond the Algorithm: Developing Evaluative Agency in the Age of Artificial Intelligence (Draft, 2026)
DKU Guide for Teaching and Generative AI (2023)
Published in August 2023, this document is not a policy, but a set of general recommendations for effective teaching in the age of generative AI.
CTL Recommendations for Teaching and Generative AI (2023)
This guide aims to help instructors learn about generative artificial intelligence (GenAI) tools, their capacities and uses in teaching, as well as key considerations for course redesign.
AI and Teaching Cases
DKU faculty have shared their teaching cases including pedagogical strategies, reflections, and concrete examples. We hope they can offer insights and inspiration for your own practice.
T&L at Lunch - AI Related Sessions
We have organized a series of AI-related T&L at Lunch sessions for our teaching and learning community since Spring 2023. NetID is required to view the session summary, resources and responses to exit ticket.
Resources
Ongoing collection of resources and tools available at DKU and from peer institutions and global higher education community
